user_query:
Read all of the following files from input/ and then design a complete, ready-to-launch channel partner program for our company:
- input/company_profile.json
- input/current_partners.csv
- input/deals_pipeline.csv
- input/preferences.yaml
- input/region_targets.csv

Deliverables (write all files under output/partner_program/):
1) output/partner_program/tier_structure.json — 4 tiers named Registered, Silver, Gold, Platinum with fields for: name, annual_revenue_min, annual_revenue_max (or null for open-ended), margin_percent (Registered=15, Silver=20, Gold=25, Platinum=30), mdf_percent (Registered=0, Silver=2, Gold=4, Platinum=6), support_level (strings), required_certified_reps (Registered=0, Silver=1, Gold=2, Platinum>=3). Calibrate revenue thresholds and include brief rationale notes per tier aligned to our inputs.
2) output/partner_program/scoring_model.json — A 5-dimension scoring model with exactly these dimensions and weights: Revenue Performance=30, Pipeline Generation=20, Certification Compliance=15, Customer Satisfaction=20, Engagement=15, plus thresholds {"probation": "<40", "maintain": "40-59", "grow": "60-79", "invest_heavily": "80+"}. Include a short description for how to compute each dimension from input/current_partners.csv.
3) output/partner_program/deal_registration_policy.md — A clear policy that must explicitly include all of: "Registration window: 90 days" (note extendable 30 days with justification), "Approval SLA: 48 hours", conflict resolution with "First to register wins" and a 60/40 split when direct already engaged, "Protected margin: Registered deals get full tier margin. Unregistered = 10% flat", and "No double-dipping" for inbound leads already in our CRM.
4) output/partner_program/compensation_models.json — Define models Reseller, Referral, Co-Sell, OEM/White-Label with fields: reseller_margin_min=20, reseller_margin_max=35; referral_commission_min=10, referral_commission_max=20; cosell_split_options=["70/30","60/40"]; oem_discount_min=40, oem_discount_max=60; plus notes on when to use each for our context.
5) output/partner_program/enablement_curriculum.md — A timeline with these sections and bullets: Week 1-2: Foundation (Product deep dive; ICP and positioning workshop; Demo certification; Portal access and deal registration training). Week 3-4: Sales Readiness (Objection handling; Competitive positioning; Pricing and packaging walkthrough; First joint call). Month 2-3: Independence (Solo demo certification; First registered deal; Co-marketing campaign launch; QBR cadence established). Tailor where appropriate to our ICP and ACV.
6) output/partner_program/co_marketing_plan.csv — Header: Activity,Cost Share,Expected Leads,Partner Effort. Include rows for exactly: Joint webinar (Cost Share 50/50), Co-branded content (Cost Share "You create, they distribute"), Event sponsorship (Cost Share "MDF-funded"), Case study (Cost Share "You produce"), Partner directory listing (Cost Share "Free"). Include realistic numeric lead ranges where relevant.
7) output/partner_program/qbr_template.md — A practical agenda using these seven sections: Scorecard Review; Pipeline Walk; Win/Loss Analysis; Enablement Gaps; Co-Marketing Plan; Growth Plan; Escalations. Include prompts and example metrics to review per section.
8) output/partner_program/program_economics.json — Include fields: recruitment_cost_min (2000-5000), recruitment_cost_max, enablement_cost_min (3000-8000), enablement_cost_max, ongoing_mgmt_per_quarter_min (500-1500), ongoing_mgmt_per_quarter_max, break_even_deals (between 2 and 3), channel_revenue_target_year3_percent=30, and rationale linking to our ACV and conversion assumptions derived from input files.
9) output/partner_program/conflict_resolution_matrix.md — Include the four scenarios: Partner vs. direct rep on same account (timeline 48 hrs), Two partners on same account (timeline 24 hrs), Partner poaching another's customer (timeline Immediate), Direct team targeting partner accounts (timeline Same day). Provide steps and escalation paths.
10) output/partner_program/partner_scorecards.csv — Compute per-partner quarterly scores from input/current_partners.csv using the scoring_model.json weights. Header: partner_name,revenue_score,pipeline_score,certification_score,csat_score,engagement_score,total_score,action_recommendation. action_recommendation must be one of: probation, maintain, grow, invest heavily, based on the thresholds in scoring_model.json. Ensure every partner from input/current_partners.csv appears here.
11) output/partner_program/launch_checklist.md — The 11-item launch checklist with checkboxes, all items present and tailored notes where relevant.
12) output/partner_program/summary_readme.md — A narrative summary (500-900 words) linking the above artifacts to our company_profile.json, preferences.yaml, deals_pipeline.csv, and region_targets.csv. Call out key choices (tier thresholds, compensation mix, MDF allocations, enablement focus) and why they fit our ACV/ICP and regional targets. Include explicit references to the created file paths.
13) output/partner_program/assumptions.md — Any assumptions you made due to missing or ambiguous input data, and how to validate them in the first 30 days.

Important:
- Use only the provided inputs under input/.
- Calibrate the program to our ACV, ICP, target regions, and preferences.
- Do not use placeholders. All numbers must be concrete and internally consistent.
- Write exactly the files above to output/partner_program/ with the specified filenames.
